title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Conservation efforts have dramatically (17) biodiversity in many REGIONS (REGION). Today, the (18) commitment to protecting nature is constantly evolving as governments and INDIVIDUALS (INDIVIDUAL) recognize the (19) importance of maintaining ecological balance CRITIC (CRITIC). These strategies include habitat restoration, (20) legislation, and global awareness campaigns EFFECT (EFFECT). Successful conservation outcomes often depend on the (21) cooperation of various organizations and countries COLLABORATE (COLLABORATE). || However, achieving these goals is not without challenges. Many species still face (22) threats due to habitat destruction and pollution SIGNIFICANT (SIGNIFICANT). There are also (23) beliefs about economic development that can undermine conservation efforts. Guided by these principles, (24) organizations strive to promote harmony between human needs and the preservation of the natural world CONSERVE (CONSERVE). Education plays a vital role in building an (25) informed populace, ready to support and engage in sustainable practices KNOW (KNOW). As awareness grows, there is hope that together, we can secure a promising future for generations to come.

example.explanation: Adverb required to modify the main verb; TRADITION → TRADITIONALLY.

questions.explanation: Noun required for plural context; REGION → REGIONS.
Noun in plural form needed due to subject context; INDIVIDUAL → INDIVIDUALS.
Adjective needed to describe significance; CRITIC → CRITICAL.
Adjective required to describe legislation; EFFECT → EFFECTIVE.
Noun needed to describe act of working together; COLLABORATE → COLLABORATION.
Adjective needed for describing types of threats; SIGNIFICANT is already in correct form.
Noun required to describe act of conserving; CONSERVE → CONSERVATION.
Noun required to describe act of conserving; CONSERVE → CONSERVATION.
